[2018-04-30 20:19] supergreyman (Greyman): Ok, I'll see how I can edit my comment. You can add additional comment if it's insufficient.
[2018-04-30 20:20] http404error: I don't really mind the unit that much anyways
[2018-04-30 20:20] http404error: I'm more concerned that multiple submissions are basically just absorbers, haha
[2018-04-30 20:22] supergreyman (Greyman): But what I meant to say is that for future reference, you can add that SDR note in your next unit design post.
[2018-04-30 20:24] http404error: I'll consider it but it will take more than that to convince me to do so
[2018-04-30 20:25] supergreyman (Greyman): I guess the only way to convince you is to have 10 more SDR-breaking submissions. 😄
[2018-04-30 20:26] supergreyman (Greyman): Either way it's fine for now.
[2018-04-30 20:26] kaelii: Fractal Rectifier: ( 2 :Gold: :Conduit: :Engineer: , supply 4 ) 7 :Fragile: 
:Blocker: , 2 :Stamina:
Start of turn: Lose up to 2 :Fragile: , gain :Gold: :Green:  (If at 1, goes to 0, dies and still produces)
:Click: 3 :Gold: :Green: (:Energy: ?), gain 4 :Fragile: (Max 11)
[2018-04-30 20:27] liadahlia (Lia): ~8 gold for 5 health of soak is pretty nutty
[2018-04-30 20:28] liadahlia (Lia): actually more like 6 gold
[2018-04-30 20:28] http404error: The problem, DDarkray, is that there are a whole bunch of commonly accepted good design principles, far more than there's room for in a weekly post
[2018-04-30 20:28] http404error: This would be a better fit for the Subreddit wiki
[2018-04-30 20:28] http404error: Which I could then just link to
[2018-04-30 20:29] http404error: SDR isn't even the most important one of those principles
[2018-04-30 20:29] http404error: @Kaelii That's a very complicated unit, I don't really understand why it has so much going on
[2018-04-30 20:30] http404error: Aegis is in fact pretty nutty also though
[2018-04-30 20:30] kaelii: Core is to convert a conduit into a defensive unit without necessarily immediately sacrificing green production if your opponent is flexing their attack.
[2018-04-30 20:30] supergreyman (Greyman): All these SDR talks in the past is still burned into brain. 😄
[2018-04-30 20:30] supergreyman (Greyman): Time to start purging them.
[2018-04-30 20:30] http404error: @Kaelii I would just make the click produce G at the cost of hp then
[2018-04-30 20:31] http404error: The healing seems unneeded
[2018-04-30 20:31] http404error: You can always just build another one
[2018-04-30 20:32] supergreyman (Greyman): @Kaelii I agree, it does have a lot of moving parts.
[2018-04-30 20:34] kaelii: If the green is moved onto the click then click is the decision between getting an extra green or having your defense up. I feel like people would often just rebuy their conduit instead of clicking and sacrificing HP. With green production and health loss mandatory the click becomes a choice between giving up your 'sacrificed' green production for more defense next turn or having defense and green this turn.
[2018-04-30 20:35] http404error: I guess! I don't really know how it would play out
[2018-04-30 20:35] http404error: Having 4 gold to spare is relatively uncommon though 😛
[2018-04-30 20:36] kaelii: True -- but if you can spend 6+engineer for a 5-7 health soak while maintaining green production that seems like a bargain bin.
[2018-04-30 20:38] supergreyman (Greyman): From what I understand, it's a resource generating machine that also acts as a blocker, but its blocking capability determinates over time and can be regenerated by spending gold and green.
[2018-04-30 20:39] supergreyman (Greyman): I would first get rid of stamina because it's just an added complexity IMO.
[2018-04-30 20:39] supergreyman (Greyman): Is there a reason for it?
[2018-04-30 20:40] kaelii: If removing stamina the heal gets reduced probably? I guess it might still be fine as a weird soak?
[2018-04-30 20:40] supergreyman (Greyman): And also, if it has 1 HP, what will happen?
[2018-04-30 20:40] kaelii: Intention would be it dies at 1 HP.
[2018-04-30 20:41] kaelii: Well dies at start of turn if at 1 HP and hasn't lost HP yet.
[2018-04-30 20:42] supergreyman (Greyman): I think something like this needs to be written out if you want to have that kind of effect. But that will take up a sentence.
[2018-04-30 20:42] http404error: That's another reason I think making it a click is better
[2018-04-30 20:47] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, The unit panel would look cleaner if the Start of turn ability and click ability is switched.
[2018-04-30 20:47] kaelii: I don't want it to be able to regenerate forever so it definitely would not get start of turn healing.
[2018-04-30 20:47] kaelii: Because then it is a pseudo absorber like mahar.
[2018-04-30 20:48] supergreyman (Greyman): But it wouldn't be like Mahar since it produces resources, not attack.
[2018-04-30 20:49] kaelii: Its still a pseudo-absorber. You take 4 on one, let it heal up and then do it again.
[2018-04-30 20:50] kaelii: All it needs to do, to act as an absorber is heal the damage it receives eventually, forever.
[2018-04-30 20:52] kaelii: Imagine if it healed 1 per turn cost 2+Conduit+Engineer and now P2 natural conduit has an immediate pseudo absorber option for 2 gold to answer the first 1-2 attackers built by the opponent.
[2018-04-30 20:54] kaelii: With a finite, degenerating health pool it can soak a bit of damage while producing green for a few turns or it can soak a larger amount of damage and give up green production.
[2018-04-30 21:03] supergreyman (Greyman): Ok, I think I'm starting to see what you mean. Generally for something like this, I would use Life Span since it preserves HP while still acting as a countdown.
[2018-04-30 21:03] kaelii: With click for green: Less health to soak later but more green to spend -- Clicking maybe a gambit. Otherwise is simply a health pool, possibly exploitable.
With Green per turn, click to gain extra health: harder to click, threat is more effective if value per turn is less than lost health value when not immediately getting soak value.
[2018-04-30 21:05] kaelii: It is fairly similar to a lifespan counter yes, this implementation is weaker to threat
[2018-04-30 21:05] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): 8 -> 5 non-prompt hp and 1g.  Clicking it seems like a waste? 1 click becomes 8+2-> 7 bt2 hp and 1g
[2018-04-30 21:06] kaelii: Its a hard space to figure out idea is to put it somewhere that you would want to click it sometimes as a gambit to extend lifespan.
[2018-04-30 21:07] kaelii: Maybe drop loss per turn to 1 and starting health down some so the heal is better value?

[2018-05-01 20:48] kaelii: Chronovore: ( 6 :Gold: :Red: :Blue: , supply 10 ) 2 :Health: 
On your turn: :Attack: 
:Click:, :Attack: :Attack: :Exhaust: 2
[2018-05-01 20:49] kaelii: Probably too inexpensive? Wasn't sure if it should be blocker or not.
[2018-05-01 20:50] liadahlia (Lia): i guess since it has blue, you would never use it as a blocker anyways
[2018-05-01 20:50] liadahlia (Lia): so basically you always click this unit?
[2018-05-01 20:50] liadahlia (Lia): sorta like a stronger iso
[2018-05-01 20:51] kaelii: Well, clicking is 1.5 damage per turn, not clicking is 1 damage per turn, so you want to click but its also not terrible to hold it a turn to synch up or something.
[2018-05-01 20:51] liadahlia (Lia): i'd like it more if it was cheaper and only give 1 sword on click
[2018-05-01 20:51] liadahlia (Lia): it's more interesting that way
[2018-05-01 20:52] kaelii: I mean if it is cheaper with 1 sword on click its basically an immolite+tarsier
[2018-05-01 20:53] kaelii: Or a pair of immolites? Maybe that's closer.
[2018-05-01 20:53] liadahlia (Lia): but you can hold it when you need
[2018-05-01 20:53] liadahlia (Lia): ok maybe turn: 2 swords, click for an extra sword exhaust 2
[2018-05-01 20:54] liadahlia (Lia): i think your idea is great, but i'm wondering if there's a way to make it so that clicking isn't a no brainer
[2018-05-01 20:55] kaelii: Probably not a good idea to put a cost on the click and make it even bigger.
[2018-05-01 20:55] kaelii: If possible I'd like the click to feel like 'consuming time'.
[2018-05-01 20:56] liadahlia (Lia): yeah i get what you mean
[2018-05-01 20:56] liadahlia (Lia): but at this ratio it's a no brainer to click
[2018-05-01 20:56] liadahlia (Lia): maybe turn: 2 attack, click for 3 attack exhaust 3
[2018-05-01 20:56] liadahlia (Lia): that way you're trading 4 attacks over 2 turns for 3 attack now
[2018-05-01 20:56] liadahlia (Lia): or turn: 1 attack, click 2 attack exhaust 3
[2018-05-01 20:57] kaelii: That would probably need a build time and/or cost increase because 5 is a lot of attack for one unit.
[2018-05-01 20:57] kaelii: Especially off of 2 resources.
[2018-05-01 20:57] liadahlia (Lia): trading 3 attack over 3 turns for 2 attack now
[2018-05-01 20:58] liadahlia (Lia): i agree that the shock of the former one is high
[2018-05-01 20:58] kaelii: Exhaust takes a bit to get down -- immolites are .5 damage per turn with exhaust 2 and 1 attack. Scorchillas are  1 damage per turn with exhaust 3 and 3 attack.
[2018-05-01 20:59] kaelii: So the function is "Attack/Exhaust" = Damage per turn.
[2018-05-01 20:59] liadahlia (Lia): the latter design feels a lot like scorches lol
[2018-05-01 21:00] kaelii: Yeah there already are some good units in the space.
[2018-05-01 21:00] liadahlia (Lia): i like the former design, but with higher tech
[2018-05-01 21:01] kaelii: Like scorch, immolite, isochrono, wincer.
[2018-05-01 21:02] kaelii: Ideally it would fit somewhere in a space between scorch, immolite, and hellhound.
[2018-05-01 21:04] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Turn: Block 1 Attack 2. Click: Pay 2Attack build 9 GauCha (1 bt1, 3 bt2, 5 bt3), exh3.
[2018-05-01 21:12] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): But click is pretty much a no-brainer: 2 attack this turn in exchange for 1 attack next turn?
[2018-05-01 21:14] liadahlia (Lia): @RiFT (#RIPBrowserers) which one are you talking about?
[2018-05-01 21:15] http404error: You could just make the click only add one more attack, and you would still usually click
[2018-05-01 21:15] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Kenshru's original one.
[2018-05-01 21:16] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): No one wants talk about the mess that is the Destroyer
[2018-05-01 21:16] liadahlia (Lia): yeah it's kind of a no brainer to click, but it lets you hold when needed
[2018-05-01 21:21] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): I'd rather have 3 immos than a borehole with threat 🤷 it's not like you can gambit it confidently, so you're giving up a damage (by delaying the click) and taking a damage (vs absorbing on wall) for 2 defense tempo?

[2018-05-03 08:28] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): Seasonal Conduit (3 :Gold: , Supply 4) 4 :Fragile: 
:Time: 3
Start of turn: gain :Green: :Green: :Green: and :Exhaust: 3
[2018-05-03 08:31] f300xen (f0x): aren't there 4 seasons
[2018-05-03 08:32] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): 4 supply 🤔 i guess exhaust 4 would be better flavor
[2018-05-03 08:35] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): 4-0-0-0 is tough to balance since bt3 is like 3.6 gold and bt4 is like 2.8
[2018-05-03 08:35] leary93: is there an actual price linked to BT?
[2018-05-03 08:37] masn6811 (Masne98888): he prolly just did inflation calculations
[2018-05-03 08:38] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): and I'd like to get it in the 3-3.2 window
[2018-05-03 08:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): i don't like seasonal conduit, because best case scenario it only saves you 1 gold
[2018-05-03 08:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): so seems pretty pointless to have in the advanced set
[2018-05-03 08:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): mobile animus by contrast saves 2 gold sometimes, and red games tend to be a lot sharper, and it also turns into a rhino
[2018-05-03 08:39] masn6811 (Masne98888): so it's a lot more an impactful unit, and i like units that are at least reasonably impactful
[2018-05-03 08:39] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): agreed, it isn't as impactful as moba/filter.  My hope was that being priced at 3 would let you sneak a conduit into some lines that you couldn't previously
[2018-05-03 08:40] leary93: mobile animus also consequently opens up some degenerate Iceblade/Hannibul rushes
[2018-05-03 08:41] f300xen (f0x): slightly more relevantly, ampo/fang
[2018-05-03 08:41] kaelii: Green exhaust conduits are kinda weird because they are either worse than conduit/s, neutral compared to conduit, or technically inflation positive.
[2018-05-03 08:42] masn6811 (Masne98888): i don't really like green exhaust conduits, because green was storable anyways
[2018-05-03 08:43] masn6811 (Masne98888): so it doesn't do anything really interesting compared to the regular one, at most save a gold when your build lines up perfectly
[2018-05-03 08:43] kaelii: Whereas blastforge/animus can actually make your tech better and worse simultaneously because you get potentially better units the on turn and maybe have difficulty finding good uses for gold the off turns.
[2018-05-03 08:44] kaelii: So if you want an odd conduit, you're probably looking at some kind of internal green dump for conversion.
[2018-05-03 08:45] kaelii: And we have a bunch of those effects already so finding a new space could be really hard.
[2018-05-03 08:51] leary93: Is the design goal actually to create an "odd conduit" or more to create something flavorish?

Which is a pretty good deal for 1.5 conduits
[2018-05-03 09:02] leary93: I feel 4 and 5 are superdangerous numbers for green production, because they either favor p1 or p2 way too much

[2018-05-03 09:19] leary93: okay, so what happens if p2 mirrors zemora?
